message:
fix: eliminate false-positive HTTP liveness probe timeouts (#74)

Three changes to prevent spurious restart cycles:

1. Reuse a single HttpClient across probes (build_probe_http_client)
   instead of creating a new connection per probe call. Removes the
   TCP-setup overhead that caused 1s timeouts under light load.

2. Cross-reference HttpRequestRuntimeDiagnostics before counting a
   probe failure: if the server completed a real HTTP request within
   the current probe interval, treat the timeout as a false positive
   (demote to DEBUG, do not increment liveness_failures).

3. Rebuild the probe client after every server restart to avoid
   stale connections to the old listener.

Closes #74

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
